context:
Setup: 
 Ridge regression loss $$ L(w) = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n (y_i - w^\top x_i)^2 + \alpha||w||_2^2 $$
fit on $D = \{(x_i,y_i)\}$, $\alpha > 0$. Model variance is defined w.r.t. experiment repetitions.

statements.text: Model bias increases as $n$ increases.?
Model variance increases as $\alpha$ increases.?
The overfitting risk of the model reduces as $\alpha$ increases.?
Model capacity increases as $\alpha$ increases.?
The underfitting risk of the model reduces as $\alpha$ decreases.?

statements.text: The precision for Class A is greater than the recall of Class A.?
The precision for Class B is equal to the recall of Class B.?
The precision for Class C is greater than the recall of Class C.?
The recall for Class A is greater than the recall for Class B.?
The accuracy of the classifier is smaller than 0.5.?

context:
Setup: 
 $C$ = number of heads in three tosses of a coin with heads probability 2/3.

statements.text: $\mathbb{E}[C] > 2$.?
$\mathbb{E}[C^2] > \mathbb{E}[C]^2$.?
$\mathbb{E}[C^2] > \text{Var}[C]$.?

statements.text: The solid red line shows the model prediction error on the test split.?
The dashed blue line shows the model prediction error on the training split.?

context:
Setup: 
 $A$ and $B$ are events with $0 < P(A) < 1$ and $0 < P(B) < 1$.

statements.text: If A and B are disjoint, they cannot be independent.?
If A and B are independent, they cannot be disjoint.?
If $A \subset B$, then it is possible that A and B are independent.?
If A and B are independent, then it is possible that A and $A \cup B$ are independent.?
There exists A and B that satisfy $P(A \cup B) = 1$.?

context:
Setup: 
 Evaluate the following statements about the k−means clustering algorithm

statements.text: The algorithm always converges to the same cluster centroids regardless of its initialization.?
The algorithm can never converge to a clustering before changing the assignment of at least one data point at least once after initialization.?
The computational complexity of the algorithm is not dependent on the data dimensionality.?
The algorithm assigns each data point to a single cluster.?
The algorithm cannot output spiral-shaped clusters when k > 1 and the algorithm is run until convergence starting from a random initialization.?

context:
Setup: 
 $$ f(x) = W_2^\top \max(W_1^\top x, 0) \quad \text{for } W_1 \in \mathbb{R}^{d\times h},\; W_2 \in \mathbb{R}^{h\times 1}, \text{ where max is applied element-wise.} $$

statements.text: The predictor can reach zero training error only if the input-output relationship is perfectly linear, i.e. $\exists \beta \in \mathbb{R}^d$ s.t. $y_i = \beta^\top x_i$ for all training inputs/labels.?
The predictor corresponds to a neural network with a single hidden layer of size $h$.?
The predictor corresponds to a neural network that uses a rectified linear unit as its activation function.?
The predictor cannot map an input outside the $[0,1]$ interval no matter which values $W_1$ and $W_2$ take.?
The gradient descent algorithm is guaranteed to converge to the global minimum of the loss function $(y-f(x))^2$ if the learning rate is chosen small enough.?
The maximum likelihood estimate for $\pi$ is $\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n X_i$.?
$\mathbb{E}[X_i] = \mathbb{E}[X_j]$ for all $i \neq j$.?
$\text{Var}[X_i] > 1$.?
If $D = \{0,1,1,0,1\}$, then the likelihood of $\pi$ is $\pi^3(1-\pi)^2$.?

context:
Setup: 
 Train takes northern route (via Brussels) 60% of the time, southern route (via Strasbourg) 40%.
Arrives late 30% of the time on the northern route, 10% of the time on the southern route.

statements.text: Observing that the train is late increases the probability that it took the northern route.?
Observing that the train is late increases the probability that it took the southern route.?
If the train chose the northern and southern route with equal percentages, then $P(\text{northern}|\text{late}) > P(\text{southern}|\text{late})$.?
If the train is observed to arrive Berlin late, then the probability that it took the northern route is smaller than the probability that it took the southern route.?
The probability that the train will arrive Berlin late is greater than the probability that it will arrive Berlin late given that it took the northern route.?

context:
Setup: 
 Assume all conditions other than those specified in each statement are equal. 
 $k$ denotes the number of nearest neighbors used for classification.

statements.text: Model bias increases if the training set size grows.?
Model variance increases if $k$ grows.?
The model has higher overfitting risk if training set size shrinks.?
The underfitting risk of the model increases if $k$ grows.?
The training error is guaranteed to be zero for all $k > 0$.?

context:
Setup: Code:
for epoch in range(20):
    for inputs_batch, outputs_batch in train_dl:
        predictions_batch = model(inputs_batch.view(-1, 5))
        loss = ((predictions_batch - outputs_batch) ** 2).mean()
        loss.backward()
        optimizer.step()
        optimizer.zero_grad()

statements.text: The code is usable for the training stage of a regression task.?
The input dimensionality is five.?
The command optimizer.step() updates the gradients of the model parameters.?
The command loss.backward() updates the learnable parameters of the model.?
The code uses each data point within the set train_dl exactly 20 times for training.?

context:
Setup: Code:
model = nn.Sequential(
  nn.Linear(100, 500),
  nn.ReLU(),
  nn.Linear(500, 5),
  nn.Softmax()
)

statements.text: The code builds a neural network with a single hidden layer containing 100 neurons.?
The model assumes that the input data points have 100 features.?
The model is designed to solve a classification task with 5 classes.?
The model has more than 100000 parameters.?
The model uses the sigmoid activation function between the hidden layer and the output layer.?
